findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `Subcoalgebra.lean:180` defines only `def groupLikeSpan (g : GroupLike R C)`, while Mathlib has `GroupLike` as a subtype at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/GroupLike.lean:66` and span induction at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Span/Defs.lean:139`; the existing proof lines 183-188 just rewrites singleton span membership and uses `g.comul_eq_tmul_self` through `simp`.
findings.fix: Define the subcoalgebra spanned by a set of group-like elements, e.g. with carrier `Submodule.span R ((↑) '' s : Set C)`, prove stability by `Submodule.span_induction`, and derive the singleton `groupLikeSpan` and its finite instance as special cases.
findings.issue: `groupLikeSpan` only builds the subcoalgebra generated by one `GroupLike`, even though the proof uses only that each generator is group-like and then closes under span.

summary: The subcoalgebra core is at a reasonable level, but the group-like span API is under-generalized: the singleton construction proves a special case of the natural span-of-group-likes result.
